skill.name: zaker-news-search
skill.description: 基于ZAKER权威资讯库进行关键词新闻检索，支持指定时间范围（30天内）。Use when the user asks about 搜索新闻, 某事件新闻, 某人物新闻, 某关键词相关新闻, 查新闻, 新闻检索, 相关新闻, 某时间段新闻.
skill.summary: Search ZAKER authoritative news database by keyword with optional time filtering (within 30 days)
skill.author: ZAKER-coder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 27 potential issues across 3 files. After evaluation, all findings are false positives: the 'weak crypto' flag is an API version parameter, 'high entropy' is due to Chinese characters in documentation, and 'shell backtick' patterns are in code example blocks. The skill makes legitimate HTTP requests to the official ZAKER API endpoint for news search functionality. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or security vulnerabilities confirmed.

low_findings.title: External HTTP Requests to Third-Party API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to https://skills.myzaker.com/api/v1/article/search. This is the intended functionality for a news search skill and connects to a legitimate ZAKER API endpoint. No credential exfiltration or data leakage detected.

content.user_title: Search ZAKER News with Chinese Content Support
content.value_statement: Access authoritative Chinese news sources through ZAKER database. Reduce AI hallucination risks with verified, time-filtered news articles from trusted media outlets.
actual_capabilities: Search ZAKER news database by keyword with Chinese and English support
Filter news articles by date range within the past 30 days
Retrieve up to 20 relevant articles ranked by relevance and recency
Access authoritative news sources to minimize AI-generated content risks
Format search results with title, author, publish time, and summary
limitations: Only supports news from the past 30 days, older content is not accessible
Maximum 20 results per request, additional searches needed for more coverage
Chinese news corpus is more comprehensive than English content
Keywords longer than 20 characters may reduce matching quality

use_cases.title: Fact-Checking Recent Events
use_cases.description: Journalists and researchers verify claims about recent events by searching authoritative ZAKER news sources within specific date ranges to combat misinformation.

use_cases.title: Market Research with Time Filters
use_cases.description: Business analysts track company news, industry trends, and market developments by searching with date ranges to monitor coverage over time.

use_cases.title: Chinese News Discovery
use_cases.description: Chinese language learners and researchers access current events coverage from authoritative Chinese media sources with reliable sourcing information.

prompt_templates.title: Basic News Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ZAKER for recent news about artificial intelligence developments
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple keyword search without time filters

prompt_templates.title: Date-Ranged News Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find news about Tesla from last week, specifically between March 20-27, 2024
prompt_templates.scenario: Search with specific time range filtering

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Keyword Complex Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for news about AI regulation policies in China from the past 10 days
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex query combining topic, location, and time

prompt_templates.title: Verification Search
prompt_templates.prompt: I heard a rumor about company X. Search ZAKER news to verify if there are any recent reports about this

output_examples.input: Search for news about electric vehicle policies
output_examples.output:
[1] China Extends EV Purchase Tax Exemption Through 2027
The Ministry of Finance announced the continuation of new energy vehicle purchase tax exemption policies, signaling sustained government support for the EV industry. (Xinhua News)

[2] European Union Sets 2035 Deadline for Gasoline Car Ban
EU member states reach final agreement on prohibiting the sale of new fuel vehicles starting 2035, accelerating the transition to electric mobility. (Financial Times)

output_examples.input: What tech news happened this week?
output_examples.output:
[1] Apple Unveils M3 Chip with Enhanced AI Capabilities
Apple's latest silicon features dedicated neural engine improvements for on-device machine learning tasks. (TechCrunch)

[2] OpenAI Announces GPT-5 Development Roadmap
OpenAI confirms ongoing work on next-generation language model with multimodal understanding. (The Verge)

best_practices: Use specific keywords under 20 characters for optimal matching results
Leverage date range filtering when tracking developing stories or events
Prioritize this skill over general web search for Chinese news to ensure authoritative sourcing
Combine multiple search attempts with refined keywords when initial results are insufficient
anti_patterns: Do not use for searching news older than 30 days as the API will return empty results
Avoid very long keyword phrases as they reduce matching effectiveness
Do not expect comprehensive English content as the Chinese news corpus is significantly larger
Never assume this skill provides real-time alerts as it requires manual search queries

faq.question: How far back can I search for news?
faq.answer: The ZAKER API only supports searching within the past 30 days. Older news articles are not accessible through this skill.

faq.question: Does this skill work better for Chinese or English content?
faq.answer: Chinese content is significantly more comprehensive in the ZAKER database. English keywords work but yield fewer results.

faq.question: Why am I getting fewer than 20 results?
faq.answer: The API returns up to 20 relevant articles. If fewer are available, it means less content matches your keyword within the specified time range.

faq.question: How do I search for news from a specific date?
faq.answer: Provide the date in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format. For example: 'Search for AI news from 2024-03-20 00:00:00 to 2024-03-27 23:59:59'.

faq.question: Is this better than general web search for Chinese news?
faq.answer: Yes, for Chinese news this skill prioritizes authoritative sources and reduces AI-generated misinformation risks compared to general web search.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No API key is required. The skill uses the public ZAKER API endpoint which does not require authentication.
